entry.title: Re: How to read a measure value into SAC Advanced Story s...
entry.content:
Hi ,

please do below @innokeyits , please accept ans/like/vote if its helpul

var data = Chart.getDataSource().getResultSet()	;
var Gross = data[0]['@MeasureDimension'].formattedValue;
Input_Message.setValue(data[0]["Location_4nm2e04531"].description);
for (var i = 0; i < data.length; i++) {
 
var v2 = data[i]['@MeasureDimension'].formattedValue;	
 
if ( Number.parseInt(Gross) < Number.parseInt(v2) ) {
	 console.log(Gross);
	Gross = v2;
	 console.log(Gross);
var v3 = data[i]["Location_4nm2e04531"].description;
console.log(v3);
	Input_Message.setValue(data[i]["Location_4nm2e04531"].description);
 
}}

entry.title: Re: Data getting erased in quickmodel of signavio(using p...

Good day Yok1,

create a support case using SAP for Me (log in with S-User ID and password), as described in this video, to have an expert look into this specific issue.

 

If you have additional questions in creating a support case, you can find more details in the SAP Signavio Onboarding Resource Center. 

 

Kind regards,

 

KantriceD

entry.title: Re: BP is not creating with respect to new customer creation
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Customer Master Data (KNA1) and Business Partner (BP) are tightly integrated, and the Business Partner is the leading object.

Therefore, when working with S/4HANA Cloud (particularly the Public Edition), you must use the Business Partner API or interfaces to create/update customer data not the older direct customer-related APIs like CMD_EI_API.

 

Why your approach didn't create the BP

- CMD_EI_API=>MAINTAIN_DIRECT_INPUT is part of the classic customer creation APIs, typically used in ECC systems.

- In S/4HANA, using this class might still update legacy tables like KNA1, but it does not trigger the necessary synchronization with the Business Partner framework, so the BP is not created or updated.

- SAP has moved to a Business Partner-centric model where customer/vendor roles are added to a BP.

 

Correct Approach for S/4HANA Cloud (especially Public Edition)

To correctly create or update a customer, including the BP:

Use the Business Partner API:

- API Name: API_BUSINESS_PARTNER

- Service Name: API_BUSINESS_PARTNER (OData V2)

You can use the service to:

    • Create Business Partner
    • Add Customer role (FLCustomer)
    • Maintain addresses, company code, sales area data, etc...

 

Example Steps (via API or BAdI)

  1. Create Business Partner with general data
  2. Assign Customer Role (FLCustomer) to BP
  3. Add Company Code & Sales Area Data

This ensures the customer is correctly created in both KNA1 and the BP framework.

 

Alternatives (if working within SAP-internal logic or extensions):

- Use BAdIs or custom logic with the standard BP API or CDS views

- Use the Business Partner replication framework if integrated with external systems (like MDG or non-SAP CRM)

 

Here’s a summary

Do not use CMD_EI_API in S/4HANA Cloud for customer creation. Instead, use the API_BUSINESS_PARTNER service to ensure proper creation of both the Business Partner and associated customer roles.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: System Access
entry.content:
Hello Omar-Javaid, 

the business demo users (eg. S4H_FIN_DEM and others) usually are lacking the credentials for such system changes indeed.

You can log on with DDIC/SAP* or BPINST and use those to assign further authorizations or make the changes directly. 
As usual, please be aware that if you change fundamental system properties, you might face problems in running the system.  

Best, 

Joerg

entry.title: SAP Sales & Service Cloud v2 + ESM APIs = ETag header for client-side HTTP caching
entry.content:
This blog article is primarily aimed at developers who are looking to enhance their understanding of the ETag and If-Match headers. By ensuring the correct implementation of these headers, developers can effectively update field values in SAP Sales Cloud and Service Cloud & ESM APIs. This approach not only guarantees data integrity but also helps reduce the high number of concurrent API usages, leading to improved performance and reliability.

ETags & If-Match

  1. The Developer makes a GET request to the CNS server.
  2. The server responds with a 200 OK status, including the content requested and an ETag header.
  3. The client caches the response and the ETag value.
  4. For subsequent requests to the same resource, the client includes the If-Match header with the ETag value it has cached while updating the field value or the record.
  5. The server regenerates the ETag independently and checks if the ETag value sent by the client matches the generated one.
    • If they match, the server responds with a 200 OK  Modified status, indicating that the client’s cached version is valid, and the field is updated with request body is sent in PATCH request.
    • If they don’t match, the server responds with a 412 Precondition Failed status, indicating that the request conditions were not met or 428 status, Request sent is incorrect

How If-Match works with ETag to prevent mid-air collisions during simultaneous updates.

Explanation:

  • Client1 and Client2 both make GET requests to the server and receive the same ETag value ("12345").
  • Both clients cache the response along with the ETag value.
  • When both clients attempt to update the resource simultaneously, they include the If-Match header with the cached ETag value.
  • The server regenerates the ETag independently for each request.
    • If the ETag matches, the server updates the resource and responds with a 200 OK status.
    • If the ETag does not match (due to another update), the server responds with a 412 Precondition Failed status, preventing the update and ensuring data integrity.

This mechanism helps prevent mid-air collisions by ensuring that only one client can successfully update the resource if the ETag matches, while others receive a failure response if the resource has been modified by another client.

Practical examples of using If-Match to ensure data integrity and consistency

Example 1 : How to get ETag value using GET Method - Response Header contains "Last Modified Date"
Example 2 : How to pass ETag value to PATCH method to update the field - Response Header Matches and updates the field value with Response 200 OK
Example 3 : How to pass ETag value to PATCH method to update the field with Incorrect If Match - Response Header validates against ETag and If-Match - If not Matched Response with 412 is sent
Example 4 : How to pass ETag value to PATCH method to update the field with Incorrect Header - Response Header validates against ETag and If-Match - If Header is incorrect 428 is sent

A few key points to keep in mind:

  • Always wrap your ETag values in double quotes when sending them

  • If the client is a performing in browser (SSC V2 UI), it’ll automatically manage the cache and send conditional requests without any extra work.

 

References

https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Reference/Headers/ETag

entry.title: Re: How to Monitor Adobe Document Services (ADS) Request...
entry.content:
Hello,

In the on-premise environment, there is a related note: 3591841 - How to monitor number of ADS calls - onprem.
The report FP_ADS_PERF_LOG_ANALYZER mentioned in this note comes from note 3519313, and it turns out that this note can also be applied in the PCE environment, and the report runs successfully.

Would you like to take a look at the note?

Best Regards

Park sangyong

entry.title: Generating Billing Document Request from Service Contract
entry.content:
I’m using SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud Edition and currently working on generating a Billing Document Request (BDR) from a service contract. When the contract contains only one line item, the system creates a single BDR as expected. However, when I include multiple line items in the service contract—even if all of them share the same billing date—the system generates separate BDRs for each line item. My requirement is to generate a single BDR that includes all line items corresponding to that billing date. Could someone please guide me on how to achieve this?

Thanks.

entry.title: SAP CPI is not picking few files from Sender SFTP AL11.
entry.content: Daily around 40 files will be placed in AL11 in one of the directory and initially due to some issue with 9 files the files went to processing mode in SAP CPI for 24 hrs and they we're discarded. Since then those file names are not being picked up by CPI, we tried to change the file name they we're successful but I need the exact file names to be processed from the same directory (client requirement) and post processing they should be placed in archive folder. Is there any solution to it, Receiver is also SFTP and we're reading the file name from property and on receiver side the filename is called through property so I cannot append timestamp. client requirement is the filename should be same daily so that they should overwrite the existing file daily. Below is the SFTP detail for reference.

entry.title: Is it possible to see the reason for variance for scrap or rework in manage production order app?
entry.content:
When using app (ID F2335), Manage Production Order, is it possible to display the reason for scrap or rework?

When hoovering over the bar chart, there is a popup information box, showing the number for rework (or scrap) Is there a way to show the reason here as well? Many thanks Adrian

entry.title: How to administrate change documents?
entry.content:
The below Change Document data needs to be retained/archived:

User Change Data:

  • Change history for logon data - USH02
  • Change history for authorizations - USH04
  • Change history for authorization profiles - USH10
  • Change history for authorization values - USH12

 

Role Change Data:

  • CDHDR
  • CDPOS


Kindly advise on how the data can be transferred as CDHDR, CDPOS also contains Change logs from another table as well.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to administrate change documents?
entry.content:
Please read the below contents carefully regarding the administration of Change Documents:

  • SAP Note 419933 - FAQ: Maintenance of change documents in user administration
  • KBA 3015823 - CD: Archiving change documents - How To

entry.title: Creating Conditions for Alternative Order Units in Info Records
entry.content:
I need to create or update purchasing info records along with their associated conditions. I'm currently using the function module ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI, which works well in most cases. However, in some situations, I need to add a condition for an alternative order unit (UoM).

 

From what I’ve observed, conditions are stored in table A067, and each condition entry generates a new KNUMH (condition record number) for the corresponding order unit (BSTME).

The issue is that this FM always applies the condition based on the EINA-BSTME (standard order unit) field. I haven't found a way to assign a condition to a different UoM.

I've also tried the following function modules, but they didn’t fulfill the requirement:

  • CND_PRICES_DETAILS_SAVE

  • ME_UPDATE_INFORECORD_COND

Does anyone know of a function module or method that allows conditions to be created for an alternative UoM in a purchasing info record?

entry.title: Re: In MRP Live can you run MRP as quick as MD03 in Class...
entry.content:
Hello @MRO-Tim-McLain 

You can link MD01N (MRP Live) to MD04 with a navigation profile definition - SPRO Production -> Material Requirements Planning -> Evaluation -> Define Navigation Profiles, e.g.

Then MD01N button is visible in MD04 with the navigation profile SAPPPMRP00:

and MD01N is called when the button is pushed. The material number and the plant are transferred to the MD01N selection screen.

Of course you can use a custom navigation profile if you don't want to change the standard SAPPPMRP00.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: IPS Job finished with error: error=The request to create an entity has failed with: 409 Conflict 'A
entry.content:
Dear all, 

 

Dear all, I have the following use case: I ran an IPS job, and it finished with an error. The reason is that a user was already created before the job ran. Is there a solution to avoid this problem? I discovered that I need to delete the user: 2907578 - Failed or missing users during IPS provisioning - User profile with email [<name>@<domain>] already exists | SAP Knowledge Base Article

Can the IPS job recognize the user and simply update it?

Thanks for your support.

entry.title: Configure SAP CPI Mail Adapter with Outlook SMTP using OAuth2 Authentication
entry.content:
1. Introduction
This guide provides a detailed step-by-step procedure to configure the SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) Mail Adapter to send emails using Outlook SMTP with OAuth2 authentication.
2. Prerequisites
Before proceeding, ensure you have the following:
• Access to an SAP CPI tenant
• Outlook account with access to Azure AD (Azure
Active Directory)
• Administrator privileges in Azure AD
• Client ID and Client Secret for OAuth2
authentication
• Access to the SAP CPI Web UI

3. Create a New App Registration in Azure AD
• Step 1: Register a new app in Azure Active
Directory.

  • Go to the Azure Portal: portal.azure.com.

Scroll down and find App resistration -

Navigate to Azure Active Directory > App Registrations

> New Registration.

 

Click on App resistration -

Create app > Fill details -

Fill in the following fields:

  • Name: Enter a name for the app (e.g., "SAP CPI Mail Adapter").
  • Supported account types: Select Accounts in this organizational directory only.
  • Redirect URI: Set the URI to

https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/nativeclient (or another relevant URI as needed).

Click Register.

App is Created –

Step 2: Get the Client ID and Tenant ID

  1. After the registration, copy the Application (client) ID and Directory (tenant) ID. These are required for authentication.

     

    Step 3: Generate a Client Secret
  2. Go to Certificates & Secrets in the app registration.
  3. Under Client secrets, click New client secret.
  4. Add a description (e.g., "SAP CPI Mail Secret") and set the expiration.
  5. Click Add and copy the Value of the client secret. This is your Client Secret.

Create New +

Copy Secret value it will be used at the time of creating OAuth 2 Authentication Code configuration

5.   Configure SAP CPI Mail Adapter

Step 1: Navigate to the SAP CPI Web UI

  1. Go to the SAP CPI tenant.
  2. Open the Integration Flow (iFlow) that you want to configure for sending emails.

Step : Set up Mail Adapter

  1. Add the Mail Adapter to your iFlow.
  2. In the Mail Adapter settings:
    • Set SMTP Server to smtp.office365.com.
    • Set Port to 587.
    • Set Security to STARTTLS.

Step 3: Configure OAuth2 Authentication

  1. In the Authentication Type dropdown, select OAuth2.
  2. Configure the OAuth2 Authorization Code Credential with the following details:
    • Authorization URL:

https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant_id}/oauth2/v2.0/authorize

 

  • Token URL: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant_id}/oauth2/v2.0/token

 

  • Client ID: Enter the Application (client) ID from the Azure AD registration.

 

  • Client Secret: Enter the Client Secret you generated in Azure AD.

 

  • Scope:

Enter https://graph.microsoft.com/.default.

 

  • https://outlook.office.com/SMTP.Send ofline_access

 

Fill all fileds –

Grant Permissions: Ensure that the Outlook account has the necessary permissions to send emails (via Mail.Send).

Add These permissions-

6.   Troubleshooting

Common Errors and Solutions

  • Error: AADSTS65001: The user or administrator has not consented to use the application
    • Solution: Ensure that the admin consent is granted in Azure AD.
  • Error: SendAsDeniedException
    • Solution: Ensure that the correct account is being used to send emails and that permissions are set correctly in Azure AD.

7.   Conclusion

  • Now you have successfully configured the SAP CPI Mail Adapter with Outlook SMTP using OAuth2 authentication. This setup enables secure email communication through the Outlook service, using the recommended OAuth2 protocol.

Appendix

  • Important URLs:
    • Azure AD Portal
    • SAP CPI Documentation
    • Microsoft Graph API

NOTE –

These Can be done with only Administrative account of MS 365 only and for both accounts Business Account (@company.com or @School.com) and Personal Account (@outlook.com) settings are different!

Check Authentication for the Email -

 For Personal account if you are a free user then you have to change it manually after this from manifest !

 

Thanks,
Dushyant

entry.title: Re: Custom Consumption Complex CDS view to Enable the CDC
entry.content: Check out the following SAP blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/cds-based-data-extraction-part-ii-delta-handling/ba-p/13425761

entry.title: Re: ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI - DBSQL_DUPLICATE_KEY_ERROR
entry.content: The Note: 2810832 - Condition save occurring twice when using ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN is already implemented in our system and does not resolve the issue, since it is the one that add those extra checks on call stack before the RV_CONDITION_SAVE.

entry.title: 📅 Day 3/30 – Master Data Essentials in SAP PP 🧩📚🏭
entry.content:
In SAP PP, master data is the foundation upon which all planning, execution, and control processes are built. Just like you can’t build a house without bricks, you can’t run efficient production without properly maintained master data! 🏗️💡

Here's a quick overview of the core master data objects in SAP PP:

🔹 📦 Material Master
The material master contains information on all the materials your company produces, procures, stores, or sells. It impacts everything — from production to purchasing and inventory.

🔹 📘 Bill of Materials (BOM)
The BOM is a structured list of components needed to manufacture a product. Think of it as a recipe for manufacturing — including materials, subassemblies, and quantities.

🔹 ⚙️ Work Centers
These represent the physical locations where production operations are performed (e.g. machines, assembly lines). Work centers include key data such as capacity, scheduling formulas, and cost centers.

🔹 📋 Routings
Routings define the sequence of operations required to produce a material. They are essential for planning lead times, capacity, and costing.

✅ Pro Tip:
Accurate and consistent master data is crucial for successful MRP runs, production orders, and cost calculations. Invest time in setting it up right — your downstream processes will thank you! 🔍✅

💬 Do you manage master data in SAP? What’s the most challenging part in your experience — BOM changes, routing accuracy, or material classification?

👇 Let me know in the comments!

entry.title: Asset Retirement Obligation (ARO) in S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Background:

Asset Retirement Obligation (ARO) in SAP S/4HANA refers to the financial responsibility a company needs to restore or retire an asset at the end of its useful life, often due to legal or contractual requirements. This obligation is typically recorded as a liability in financial statements and is accounted for under various accounting standards, such as US GAAP and IFRS.

 

In this article let's try to understand the ARO process in it's lifecycle, what are the master data, transactional data objects involved and how each process are connected to asset accounting and General Ledger, specifically on the accounting principles and ledgers for respective accounting standards.

In SAP S/4HANA, ARO is managed through structured processes that include:
- ARO Configuration: Setting up the integration between ARO and fixed asset accounting.
- ARO Creation and Release: Defining asset classes, cost objects, and contract durations.
- Accretion Posting: Monthly accruals to reflect the increasing liability over time.
- Depreciation Charges: Calculating depreciation using methods like straight-line depreciation.
- Settlement/Decommissioning: Finalizing the obligation by recording the actual costs incurred.

 

I will few more articles in future which covers specific aspects about BC Sets to import initial set up, Configuration steps, migration of ARO Simple approach and detailed approach.

entry.title: Re: A Relationship (basic) of MM and FI tables
entry.content:
Hello Jao,

It's so helpful to understand the basic integration of MM/FI, thanks a lot for sharing.

Regards,

Chafik

entry.title: 🐞 SAP SuccessFactors Bug Report: Duplicate Transactions Triggered by the Escape Key
entry.content:
📌Overview

SAP SuccessFactors is one of the most robust and widely used cloud-based HCM (Human Capital Management) suites in the market today. Its modular design, including Employee Central, Time Management, Recruiting, and Performance Management, allows businesses to manage their workforce efficiently. These modules are leveraged by thousands of organizations globally to manage day-to-day HR operations.

Given this extensive reach and the business-critical nature of the application, even low-severity bugs can have widespread implications. Ensuring a seamless, intuitive experience for end-users is essential for productivity and system trust.

Recently, a low-level yet recurring issue has been observed in commonly used transactions related to time and employee data management:

  • View Time Sheet for [Employee]

  • Administer Time for [Employee]

  • View Job Information for [Employee]

  • View Name for [Employee]

  • View Employment Information for [Employee]

     

    ⚠️The Bug: Duplicate Transactions When Pressing ESC Key

    When performing tasks in View Time Sheet or Administer Time, pressing the Escape (ESC) key — a standard user behavior intended to clear transaction — triggers a duplicate instance of the transaction

    🔄Steps to Reproduce:

    1. Log in to SAP SuccessFactors.

    2. Navigate to either Administer Time or View Time Sheet.

    3. Press Escape Key After entering the Transaction

    4. Result: The same transaction is re-invoked automatically

    ‌‌Call to Action for SAP Product Team

    Given the nature of this issue and its presence in key transactional areas, it is imperative that the SAP SuccessFactors product team evaluates and addresses this behavior as part of their ongoing commitment to usability and system stability. Even minor inconsistencies in expected user interactions—such as the misuse of the ESC key—can erode user confidence and introduce inefficiencies at scale. We recommend this be flagged for review and inclusion in a future maintenance release or patch cycle.

  •  

    Screenshots of the Bug:

entry.title: Re: How to Monitor Adobe Document Services (ADS) Request...
entry.content:
Hi Park,

Thanks for the note, but I'm actually looking for a tool that works specifically in the Public Edition environment.

entry.title: HTTP 431 Error – Work Zone Admin UI Not Accessible Due to Request Header Size
entry.content:
We are experiencing a consistent 431 Request Header Fields Too Large error when accessing the SAP Build Work Zone (standard edition) Admin UI in our development subaccount.

  • The base domain returns 302 Found, redirecting to /sites

  • The 431 only happens when the browser sends a Cookie header (e.g., user session or SSO)

  • The backend CAP service is working and reachable via direct app URLs

  • The HTML5 application is deployed correctly via the Managed AppRouter

  • The issue persists across browsers and profiles, and also occurs in curl requests with cookies

entry.title: VAT on down-payments made in Public cloud
entry.content: The client requests the process to post down payment made to supplier with VAT amount. I don't see where to configure "VAT on down-payments made" G/L account. Could you advise this process in Public Cloud? Thank you.

entry.title: Re: How to improve Date Dimension labels in a line chart...

entry.title: Is it possible to refer the SAP BTP standard roles in xs-security.json?
entry.content:
Is it possible to refer the SAP BTP standard roles in xs-security.json? We have set-up role collections manually referencing the standard role, however we are unable to refer standard role in xs-security.json. Every blog/github code is creating role template and then role collection referencing that collection. However I am trying to refer SAP roles as this will save lot of time. 
Can someone please confirm if referencing SAP standard roles or role collections in xs-security.json is possible or not? If possible can someone share the code snippet how to refer the SAP standard roles or role collections in xs-security.json?

entry.title: Unable to define Custom Naming Rules for Payment Medium Files
entry.content:
I want to define a Custom Naming Rules for the Payment Medium File format.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to define Custom Naming Rules for Payment Medi...
entry.content:
DME file naming convention, file names are fix, they are generated automatically by the system hence they cannot be changed. Possible suggestion is download the file, open it and save it with the required name convention that your bank requires.

As described in SAP S/4HANA Cloud - Help Portal: Defining Custom Naming Rules for Payment Medium Files:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/031c345485b84c8c94265be9ef61d3a8/c3bf972389694cc5bb91ba78a85fc83e.html?q=Japan

entry.title: Inconsistency in PR number range. PR number suddenly get last year document number range
entry.content:
When user create PR, after user save the document , number that they get is a range From last year document number . Not current year.

 

 

 

after i search the forum. i get the situation . if the number jump forward, it is because of buffer number in snro.

but in my case. it jump backward . and if i create again suddenly  got the correct number range

Could you tell me how this situation occur ?

There is no change in number range ( SNRO=> BANF )  configuration  from the beginning implement until now

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors SoD Matrix for SAP GRC
entry.content:
Hello,

We are connection SAP SuccessFactors with SAP GRC Access Control to analyses the possible SoD conflicts.

Actually, SAP doesn't provide any standard Matrix or example on how to create it. Since it doesn't follow the permissions/accesses style of an ERP, does anyone have any matrix or examples of how permissions should be included in the tool?

Thanks in advanced,

Regards.

entry.title: Re: Make Application Job Templates available to specific user(s) only? Anno 2025
entry.content:
Hi @vincentverheyen, Job Definitions do not have an effect on IBP Excel Add-In. As a suggestion, you might consider submitting a request via the Customer Influence portal and share the link here, so we can vote and increase the chances of being accepted.

This is not very practical and it is time-consuming, but you might try restricting the access to a Shared Template via Business Roles (Permission Filter).

Hello, I am facing this problem while I have set the container to empty

 

 

Long message: 

 

 

I'm working with 1 Step Stock Transfer

entry.title: Customer & Partner Roundtable for SAP BTP ABAP Environment #23 - demo time in ADT
entry.content:
👉 The call for contributions for one of the upcoming roundtables is open! 

 

If you want to show a demo or share a use case scenario for SAP BTP ABAP Environment send us an email and we will get back to you.
 

Introduction

 

As SAP BTP ABAP environment (aka Steampunk) and ABAP Cloud became more popular inside and outside of SAP, there is a high demand for rolling out the latest product news and updates, asking questions, and showing demos. 

💡 You can find the slides presented, recordings, and further references from the previous roundtables in this GitHub repository.


Meeting Information

When:                                                                      
  • May 20th, 10:00 - 11:00 AM CET  – Zoom Meeting (please register in advance) 
 
Who:
  • All interested customers, partners, and stakeholders are invited to join this roundtable
  • BTP ABAP team:
    • Frank Jentsch (Product Lead for SAP BTP ABAP Environment)
    • Iwona Hahn (Product Management)
    • Nora Klemp (Product Management)
    • Shyam Balachandran (Development Expert)
    • Dr. Oliver Brein (Development Expert)
    • Simon Dold (Developer)
Preliminary Agenda:
  • Product update for SAP BTP ABAP Environment release 2505
  • Demos in ABAP Development Tools (ADT):
    • Quick Fiori app generation (Shyam Balachandran)
    • Swagger support for OData services (Shyam Balachandran)
    • UX improvements of generator framework (Shyam Balachandran)
    • HANA PLV tracing incl. SQL statement viewer in ADT Cross Trace (Dr. Oliver Brein)
    • Enhancements of Manage Software Components app (Simon Dold)
  • Q&A 
Looking forward to meeting you!
 


Check out our SAP Business Technology ABAP Environment page in SAP Community for product updates and upcoming events.

entry.title: Re: Import Sales Orders - Part4: Custom fields support
entry.content: Hi @EDobbelstein, sorry I am not clear about the on premise timeline. Now the three app "Import Credit Memo Requests", "Import Debit Memo Requests" and "Import Customer Returns" are available in Cloud.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office – Storage of workbooks
entry.content: hello @SteffenHeine in your screenshot of the SAC Workbook Folder with the Excel the description is not shown (I have the same behaviour in Analysis). Is there any way to show the descriptions which were added when creating the folders in SAC? Thanks, Daniel

entry.title: Re: GR/IR issue
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Selective Data Transfer to SAP Cloud ALM: Why It Matters and How to Approach It
entry.content:
Why Selective Data Transfer is Important

  • Avoid Information Overload: Not all historical data is necessary in the new environment. Selective transfer helps ensure that only useful, clean, and current information populates SAP Cloud ALM.
  • Optimize Migration Efforts: Focusing on critical data reduces time, effort, and costs during migration.
  • Stay Compliant: Some industries require traceability and documentation. Selectively transferring key records ensures continued compliance.
  • Leverage New Capabilities: SAP Cloud ALM offers streamlined, cloud-optimized capabilities. Having relevant data aligned to these features ensures maximum value from day one.

 

What Needs to Be Done

To prepare for a successful selective data transfer, organizations need to:

  1. Identify Valuable Data: Determine which solution documentation data is still actively needed.
  2. Clean Up the Solution Manager Environment: Archive or delete obsolete and redundant information to minimize noise.
  3. Define the Transfer Scope: Clearly outline what data sets should move — based on business value and future use cases.
  4. Engage with SAP Services: SAP provides tools and guided sessions to help in the evaluation and execution of the transfer.

 

How the Selective Data Transfer to SAP Cloud ALM Works

At a high level, selective data transfer typically follows these steps:

  1. Preparation Phase:
    • Analyze and prioritize data within SAP Solution Manager.
    • Use SAP Readiness Check for SAP Cloud ALM to gain an overview of transferable content.
  2. Scope Definition:
    • Define the transfer content (e.g., process hierarchy, library elements, business processes, documents and test cases).
    • Validate data quality and completeness.
  3. Data Extraction:
    • Use Selective Data Transfer application to pull the selected data from SAP Solution Manager.
  4. Data Mapping:
    • Extracted data is adapted where necessary.
    • This ensures compatibility and readiness for upload.
  5. Data Upload into SAP Cloud ALM:
    • Selected data sets are imported into SAP Cloud ALM.
    • Post-transfer validation ensures everything was migrated successfully.
  6. Post-Migration Activities:
    • Train users on the new environment with the transferred data.

 

Learn More – Expert Guided Implementation (EGI) Sessions

SAP offers Expert Guided Implementation (EGI) sessions designed to assist customers through the Selective Data Transfer process. These interactive sessions, delivered remotely by SAP experts, provide step-by-step guidance.

 

To find upcoming EGI sessions, including dates per region, visit:
👉SAP Enterprise Support Academy - Expert Guided Implementation Calendar

Don't miss the opportunity to engage early — spaces in these sessions are often limited!

 

Conclusion

The Selective Data Transfer from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM is a critical step in future-proofing your SAP landscape. By focusing on what matters most, you can streamline operations, reduce clutter, and make the most of SAP’s latest innovations. Planning early, leveraging SAP's tools and expertise, and engaging in guided sessions are key to a smooth, successful migration.

 

Are you ready to get started on your move to SAP Cloud ALM?

Hello,

I already made a rating scale in increments of 0.1, but still the same results. Decimal values still weren't accepted.

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI is not picking few files from Sender SFTP AL11.
entry.content:
Hi Nikhil,

 

Can you check probably the files that went to processing/discarded status are still part of Message Locks in Manage Locks view of Monitor Tile, that could be reason why the same filename is not getting processed as an active lock for the filename is there, this is a guess, hope it helps !

entry.title: SAP Application for Android/IOS Devices
entry.content:
Hello All,

I would like to know the opinion from Community members on building Fiori app for Android/IOS Devices. 

So far, explored the concepts of SAP Mobile Services with SAP Build(MDK Application) and SAP Business Application Studio from #https://developers.sap.com/mission.mobile-dev-kit-get-started.html

SAP Mobile Start - #https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/mobile-start-access-launchpad-site.html

Any other suggestions on building native apps using SAPUI5 library?

Thanks,

Muthu.

entry.title: Re: VAT on down-payments made in Public cloud
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

There's no proper existing configuration path equivalent in the S4 hana cloud public edition version but if you want to take into consideration VAT item while posting a down payment, here is a note explaining how to do so :

3285760 - How to create down payment request without tax code - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

What the note does not mention is that the modification has to be made at the company level in Manage G/L master data app :

I hope this will help you solve your issue.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Can ABAP optimize inequalities in LOOP AT sortedItab WHERE, like greater than, between?
entry.content:
Do you know if the below LOOP AT will be optimized and fast?

CLASS ltc_test DEFINITION FOR TESTING.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS test FOR TESTING.
    TYPES ty_itab TYPE SORTED TABLE OF i WITH NON-UNIQUE KEY table_line.
    CLASS-DATA itab TYPE ty_itab.
    CLASS-METHODS class_setup.    
ENDCLASS.

CLASS ltc_test IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD class_setup.
    DATA(integer_generator) = cl_abap_random_int=>create( seed = 
cl_abap_random=>seed( ) min = 1 max = 1000000 ).
    itab = VALUE #( FOR i WHILE i <= 1000000
                    ( integer_generator->get_next( ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD test.
    LOOP AT itab TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS
         WHERE key BETWEEN 250000 AND 260000.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Thanks

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Can ABAP optimize inequalities in LOOP AT sortedItab...
entry.content:
The optimization of LOOP AT has been improved in ABAP 7.58 to support BETWEEN, >, >=, <, <=, <>.

Before ABAP 7.58, the optimization used to work only with = and AND, so BETWEEN was not optimized.

References of WHERE optimization in ABAP Keyword Documentation:

  • Since ABAP 7.58: itab - Optimizing the WHERE Condition (Release News 7.58 - Internal Tables - Improved Optimization of the WHERE Condition)
  • Before ABAP 7.58, for instance 7.57: itab - Optimizing the WHERE Condition

entry.title: Re: Import Requisitions from S/4 Hana to Ariba
entry.content:
Hi Julia,

Have you resolved this issue. if not please check if the below feature is enabled and the Import Requisition task using managed gateway is enabled or not. Below is the feature and KBA.

CP-11262 - Support Async processing via Managed Gateway for Spend&Network integration

KB0406429 - Additionally the Webservice task Import Requisitions needs to be enabled.

let me know if your issue is resolved.

 

Regards

Suprit

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 HANA (10.00.280 FP2502) – Invalid Username or...
entry.content:
Hi Javokhir_Isroilov 

Please check if below steps helps:

  1. - Open the Windows Registry Editor, Navigate to HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\SAP\SAP Manage\SAP Business One. Right-click the SAP Business One key, select New → DWORD (32-bit) Value, name the new DWORD as "CockpitStartTime", set the value to 10 in decimal, and restart SAP Business One.
    - If the issue still is not resolved, incrementally increase the value of "CockpitStartTime" to 20, then 30.
  2. Disable the cockpit.
  3. You can try work with Single Sign On (SSO) with no issues.
  4. Latest workaround with using the lowercase username.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Revenue Recognition Key cannot be maintained for FOC item categories
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community, 

there is no possibility to set a Revenue Recognition Key for the following FOC Item Categories:

  • TADN
  • CBXN 
  • CBXB 
  • CBLN 
  • CBXL
  • CB4
  • C4L 

However, we need the possibility to set a Revenue Recognition Key for FOC Item Categories as the first Sales Order Line Item derives the Revenue Recognition Key.

Can you please let us know if this is possible? 

Thank you very much and best regards,

Annika

entry.title: Re: Automatic adjustment of date filter in rolling foreca...
entry.content: I suppose you can create a input control for cut over date with time range filter using dynamic system date.

entry.title: Re: ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI - DBSQL_DUPLICATE_KEY_ERROR
entry.content:
Thank you very much! I have check all those notes but none of them solve my issue.

Since the fm is not released and therefore not supported for custom programs, the only solution seems to be enchansment that forces the  

cl_mmpur_bapi_po=>check_repid_loaded( 'ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI==FT' )

 to return abap_true.

entry.title: How to replicate documents from BTP DMS Production to Test Tenants
entry.content:
Dear

My customer is currently utilizing SAP BTP Document Management Service (DMS) with the DMS Integration Option to manage and store documents in thier ERP systems.

They would like to inquire about the available options for replicating documents that are stored in their BTP DMS Production tenant into their Test tenant.

Specifically, They would like to understand:

  • Is there a standard method or tool provided by SAP for copying or replicating documents between BTP DMS tenants (Production → Test)? Is it better to approach this task from the ERP side or the BTP side?

  • If so, could you please provide guidance or best practices for performing this replication securely and efficiently?

  • Are there any specific prerequisites or limitations we should be aware of?

BR, Jungwoo

entry.title: Convert SAP Old code to SAP New Syntax
entry.content:
Dear Team,

How to convert the below code into sap new syntax

 LOOP AT it_h1 INTO wa_h1n WHERE regiogroup = 'S01' AND supply_division+0(1) = 'W'.
        IF wa_h-langu_corr = 'M2'.
          LOOP AT it_swm INTO lv_string WHERE opbel_bi = wa_h1n-opbel_bi.
            TRANSFER lv_string TO p_file.
            CLEAR lv_string.
          ENDLOOP.
        ELSE.
          LOOP AT it_swe INTO lv_string WHERE opbel_bi = wa_h1n-opbel_bi.
            TRANSFER lv_string TO p_file.
            CLEAR lv_string.
          ENDLOOP.
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.

Now our requirement is   dynamically it should change month from backend and fetch data to SAC 

EX: 1 Jan 25 to 31Jan 25

again next run  1Feb 25 to 28 Feb-25

Thanks,

Raghunath

 

can some one guide on same 

 

 

dyanamic   bacground period  to period  

 

every month how to 

dynamic

entry.title: Re: Entering Trading Partner in Park supplier invoice App
entry.content: As far as I know, the logistics supplier invoice document does not include a "trading partner" field, meaning you cannot enter the trading partner in the 'Parking Supplier Invoice' app. Instead, you need to use the trading partner field in the vendor master and then post the supplier invoice from a logistics perspective. Alternatively, you can use the 'Create Incoming Invoices' app from a finance perspective to post documents that include the trading partner field.

entry.title: Data volume problems during an SAP S/4HANA Conversion
entry.content:
An SAP S/4HANA conversion refers to migration from a legacy SAP ERP system, such as SAP ECC, to the modern SAP S/4HANA business suite. This process is essentially a platform upgrade that includes adapting data system configurations, and custom developments to ensure compatibility with S/4HANA. Click here to read more about the features of S/4HANA.

There are three approaches available when embarking on this journey (read a more in-depth discussion about them here)

  1. Greenfield approach - means starting from a clean slate.
  2. Brownfield approach - is more like a system upgrade.
  3. Hybrid approach - lets you select the best parts of Greenfield and Brownfield implementations.

Many customers embarking on this transformation journey opt for the brownfield conversion approach. The brownfield approach retains your historical data which can lead to the technical challenge we will discuss and solve in this blog.

SAP provides a process for the conversion to SAP S/4HANA, this is illustrated in the figure below which gives an overview of the tools, the phases, and the activities involved in the process. The activity that we will be focusing on is t5 (Software Update Manager (SUM)), specifically the “Data Conversion” task:

For further reading:

  • Conversion phases and related activities are documented in the  “Conversion Guide for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023”, which can be found under the Implement section of the SAP S/4HANA | SAP Help Portal.
  • For information specific to accounting components please refer to the following SAP note 2332030 - Conversion of accounting to SAP S/4HANA - SAP for Me.

The “Data Conversion” task is the conversion of your data into the new data structure used by SAP S/4HANA.

At this point you could be wondering “What new data structure used by SAP S/4HANA?”, well I’m glad you asked, let me explain.

The data model used in SAP S/4HANA has been simplified drastically when comparing it with the data model used in SAP ERP. If you look at the Finance section of the below diagram you will see that many of the legacy tables have been consolidated into a simplified data model focused on the ACDOCA table.

Furthermore, the effects of Accounting Data Model Changes and how they impact the conversion are described as follows in an extract from the Converting Your Accounting Components to SAP S/4HANA guide:

To use Finance in SAP S/4HANA you must migrate the existing user data from the General Ledger, Asset Accounting, Controlling and Material Ledger. The data migration is necessary because Finance in SAP S/ 4HANA rests on a uniform data model for all accounting areas. The comprehensive ACDOCA data table contains all line-item documents. After the migration, all postings of the named applications are written into the ACDOCA table.

The following tables are replaced by views using the same technical names:

  • The line item, totals tables and application index tables of the General Ledger (GLT0, BSIS, BSAS and FAGLFLEXA, FAGLFLEXT, FAGLBSIS, FAGLBSAS).
  • The totals tables and application index tables of Accounts Receivable and Accounts Payable (KNC1, KNC3, LFC1, LFC3, BSID, BSIK, BSAD, BSAK).
  • The line item and totals tables of Controlling (COEP for certain value types, COSP and COSS).
  • The material ledger tables for parallel valuations (MLIT, MLPP, MLPPF, MLCR, MLCD, CKMI1, BSIM).
  • The Asset Accounting tables (ANEK, ANEP, ANEA, ANLP, ANLC).

This is the crux of our challenge: data that was previously distributed across multiple tables in the legacy system is now consolidated into a single table in the S/4HANA environment, that is ACDOCA.

Although this may not appear to be an issue under normal circumstances, in high-volume scenarios, technical limitations of the HANA database platform can arise when a single table (or partition) approaches a very large number of records. Specifically, each table or partition in HANA is limited to a maximum of 2 billion entries.

The solution: table partitioning!

Let’s do a deep dive into partitioning and its role during conversions by exploring a few questions.  

Question 1: What is table partitioning?

According to  SAP Help Portal: The partitioning feature of the SAP HANA database splits column-store tables horizontally into disjunctive sub-tables or partitions. In this way, large tables can be broken down into smaller, more manageable parts. Partitioning is typically used in multiple-host systems, but it may also be beneficial in single-host systems.

Question 2: Why do we need to do partitioning?

As mentioned earlier, there is a limitation of 2 billion records per table (partition) on the HANA database. This is described in more detail in SAP Note 2154870 - How-To: Understanding and defining SAP HANA Limitations - SAP for Me.

If the limit is reached the following errors could be generated:

  • 2048: column store error: [17] Docid overflow in current delta.
  • 2055: maximum number of rows per table or partition reached: '<schema>.<table>'
  • Maximum number of rows per partition reached.
  • Max row count exceeded.
  • exception 1: no.3100025: Allowed rowcount exceeded.

Below is an example of a short dump with the error “maximum number of rows per table or partition reached” that can occur in step ID MUJ “Data Migration into Unified Journal: Line Items”:

 

Question 3: How do we know that we need to do partitioning for the conversion?

The Readiness Check which forms part of the Preparation Phase can guide the need for partitioning. It details the table records in the existing tables in ECC (classic General Ledger or New General Ledger), and also gives an estimate of the number of records expected in ACDOCA. See the table below as an example:

The calculation is not exact, but it already gives you an idea that the number of records will exceed 1 billion for ACDOCA, in this case, partitioning is required.

Further Reading: Great community blog - SAP S/4HANA Simplification Item Check - How to do it right.

Note: Ensure that all relevant notes are implemented to get thorough analysis and results.

Question 4: How should we partition the tables?

Luckily there is quite a bit of guidance on partitioning in general, as well as specific guidance for Finance Tables, please review the following:

  1. SAP Note 2044468 - FAQ: SAP HANA Partitioning
  2. SAP Note 2289491 - Best Practices for Partitioning of Finance Tables - SAP for Me
  3. For scale out scenarios read SAP Note 3325787 - Partition-Distribution of Financial Data in SAP S/4HANA
  4. Guidance provided in SAP Customizing Implementation Guide:

When designing a partitioning strategy, it is important to analyze how the data is distributed over Fiscal Periods and Years, and to anticipate future data growth.

Note: Pay attention to the following guidance from SAP Note 2289491 - Best Practices for Partitioning of Finance Tables when creating future partitions à Too many empty partitions can affect the HANA optimizer and cause poor performance for queries which don't apply partition pruning.

Question 5: When should partitioning be done during the conversion process?

The partitioning of the ACDOCA table, in the target system, can be done once the SUM tool has reached the “business downtime” phase (this phase is known as DOWNCONF_DTTRANS) on the source system (in SUM uptime phase).

Note 1: Other tables are partitioned automatically by the SUM tool.

Note 2: If partitioning isn’t performed before the data migration it is recommended to reset the migration rather than trying to apply partitioning with an already populated ACDOCA. Once records exist in ACDOCA, applying partitioning becomes significantly slower. It's recommended to reset the migration (Step ID MUJ is where ACDOCA begins to be populated) - this clears the records in ACDOCA — then proceed with partitioning.

Before we wrap up, we would like to mention that it is very important to focus on Data Volume Management before conversion.

Benefits are:

  1. Reduced data footprint which results in a shorter conversion duration.
  2. Reduced memory requirement for the new SAP S/4HANA system.

The following guide and notes contain useful reading on the topic:

  • 2818267 - Data Volume Management for FI/CO during migration to SAP S/4HANA
  • Data Volume Management during an SAP S/4HANA Conversion

Hope you enjoyed this blog! Please feel free to leave suggestions and comments.

This blog was co-authored by @DotEiserman.

entry.title: Re: ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI - DBSQL_DUPLICATE_KEY_ERROR
entry.content:
Too bad. So, as Raymond says, SAP has not released this function module, it should not be called by custom programs. If you still want to use it, it's at your own risk and you will have to debug and enhance the code.

If you want to find a supported way to maintain the conditions, see if batch input is possible.

entry.title: S_TABU_LIN object not being checked -> failed to load data
entry.content:
Alright, so this is a weird question, because I got it to work in one BW (SAP Business Warehouse) 4/HANA system but not in another.

The task is simple and I will simplify it even further: You have an InfoObject ZTEST with two attributes which don't matter. There are two values we will load into it using master data maintenance: 00000 and 00001. One user called USER_1 is supposed to only see and maintain the value 00000 and another user should only see and maintain the value 00001 in the master data maintenance. I want to reiterate, that I already got this to work in one system but I am clueless as to why it's not working in another one.

Now we do the spicy stuff, we use this little thing here:

The master data maintenance with authorization check. Now because I analyzed this thing for quite some time now, I know what it does. The user that logs into the cockpit is now checked against S_TABU_NAM and S_TABU_LIN. In my old system, you were also checked against S_RS_IOMAD but in the new one, you're instead checked against S_TABU_DIS for some reason, but you're not anymore if you have S_TABU_NAM already. So S_TABU_NAM gives you access to the table and suddenly it asks for S_TABU_CLI too which is fine, it wants you to be able to maintain cross-client tables, okay. Now the interesting bit is S_TABU_LIN.

When we activated the authorization check, it automatically created an activated a corresponding organization criterion that it will check against to see if you have access to the individual rows. It also only checks for 02 - Change. Now, if I put in "*" as the value in my custom role for the authorization object S_TABU_LIN, it will work fine. My test user can see all rows. If you remove S_TABU_LIN, it will say "no data" and in the authorization trace, you can see that it checked for the existence of S_TABU_LIN and didn't find it.

Everything as expected until now. If I put in 00000 as the only value in the authorization object, it doesn't display anything and instead shows "failed to load data". Looking at the trace we see... nothing. It checks for the existence of S_TABU_LIN but no further checks. In fact, the test user passes all checks the system gives it but they still don't see any data. Again, in my old system this simply worked, the test user say one line with 00000 and the other row was simply not displayed.

What can cause this? How can I get this to work? The task seems easy and standard enough to be common, but I find no information on anyone even knowing what "master data maintenance with authorization checks" actually does.

Thanks for any hint or trace on how to resolve this issue.

entry.title: Bringing Structure and Guidance to Requirement and Approval Statuses in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM continues to evolve, and the latest enhancement to the Requirements application introduces an important shift in how requirement statuses and approval states interact. Previously, users could freely update both approval and requirement statuses without restrictions, which offered flexibility but lacked process guidance. With the new capabilities, SAP Cloud ALM introduces structure, ensures alignment between approval and requirement statuses, and improves governance by controlling when requirement descriptions can be edited.

What Has Changed?

Conditional Status Selections Based on Approval State

In the past, any requirement status could be selected regardless of the approval state. This unrestricted flexibility is now replaced by a more guided approach, where available requirement statuses are determined by the requirement’s approval state. This ensures a clearer lifecycle and better control.

Approval and Requirement Status Mapping

To clearly understand how approval and requirement statuses now interact, refer to the table below:

 
Approval State

Requirement Status

No Approval Required

“In approval” no longer visible in dropdown

Selectable values are:
• In Refinement
• In Planning
• In Realization
• In Testing
• Successfully Tested
• Blocked
• Confirmed
• Not Planned

This state is best used for requirements that do not need a formal approval workflow.

 

Ready for Approval

The system sets the status to "In Approval".
No other status than “In approval” can be set until requirement is either:
• Approved
• Rejected
• Reverted to “No Approval Required”

This enforces discipline during the approval process and locks the requirement in a pending state.

Approved

The system sets the status to "In Planning" and allows you to change to:
• In Realization
• In Testing
• Successfully Tested
• Blocked
• Confirmed
• Not Planned

Once a requirement is approved, it follows a logical progression through implementation phases.

 

Rejected

The system sets the status to "Not Planned"

The requirement is effectively paused and marked as not moving forward.

 

From “Ready for Approval” to Not Assigned

(1) Requirement was in status “In Approval”
(2) Approval state changed (e.g., to “No Approval Required”)
(3) System sets status to "In Refinement"

 

 
 

Trail and Reset Capabilities

  • All changes to statuses and approval states are logged with timestamp and user information in the Requirement History.

     

  • Project Administrators can reset approved or rejected requirements to "Not Assigned", and the system will reset the status to "In Refinement". This gives flexibility when re-evaluation is needed without losing historical traceability.

 

Locking of Description Fields

Another major improvement is the locking of requirement descriptions in certain states to ensure clarity and integrity of the content:

  • Descriptions are locked when:

    • Approval State = No Approval Required or Rejected

    • Requirement Status = In Approval, Successfully Tested, or Confirmed

This prevents changes to critical requirements once they’ve reached a significant phase or decision point in the workflow.

 

Guidance Messages for Users

To support users, the system now provides real-time messages and hints when attempting status or approval changes. These messages:

  • Indicate the next possible or required action.

  • Explain why a status is locked or unavailable.

  • Help prevent invalid transitions.

 

Conclusion :

These enhancements bring much-needed structure and governance to requirements management in SAP Cloud ALM. By aligning requirement status transitions with approval states and locking critical information at the right times, the system now supports a more disciplined and auditable requirements lifecycle.

Whether you're refining your backlog or managing complex implementation phases, these updates helps ensure that your team follows a clear and consistent process from ideation to confirmation.

More Improvements are planned in the direction of bringing consistency, traceability, and clarity in requirement management. Stay tuned for more updates and guidance.

entry.title: Re: Users and dates are not being saved in my RAP applica...
entry.content:
@ronaldo_aparecido 

Please ensure that the behavior definition includes the mapping between the CDS View and the persistent table for the date and user fields.

entry.title: Re: Transitioning from SAP BW to SAP Datasphere: Key Challenges You Should Know
entry.content: I agree that the solution feels incomplete. It often seems that SAP prioritizes selling their products over ensuring a smooth customer experience, as post-sales support can be lacking and the SAP team is less visible after purchase. Additionally, I find both Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) to be not particularly user-friendly, which can make adoption and daily use challenging for end users. SAP has acknowledged customer satisfaction issues and is working to improve integration and post-sales support, but these areas still need significant enhancement to meet user expectations

entry.title: Re: How to Keep MEMBERSET Scope in DATA Function
entry.content:
Hi @ShrutiN,

The data action script is working as expected in my testing. But I am not clear about your comment  

"  When reviewing the Data Function Scope, we see the following:

    COMP_CODE: 221

    Line 17: In current statement "

How did you review the Data Function Scope? If available, please attach a screen capture of reviewing "Data Function Scope".

Regards,

Moonjun

entry.title: Re: Rebate to Payer capped on a Ship-to Customer
entry.content: I have implemented successfully item split criteria, the use of split criteria in pricing and the adaption of AMIW/AMIZ. I have not yet tried the use of the group key routine (Also known as Group Condition Routine). The group key routine is the best starting point for fulfilling your requirement. I am confident, but no success guaranteed.

entry.title: Re: Statutory Reporting France Italy
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

Have you tried the check in the SFW5 transaction code ?

Try to find something like : FIN_LOC_IT_XX (Localization Italy)

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Datasphere CLI not working
entry.content:
It looks like CLI command has changed since the recording of that video, and should include `config` like in https://www.npmjs.com/package/@sap/datasphere-cli#from-the-command-line:

datasphere config cache init -H https://mytenant.eu10.hcs.cloud.sap/ -p somepasscode

Regards.

entry.title: Re: Data volume problems during an SAP S/4HANA Conversion
entry.content: Thank you Yvonne and Dot, for sharing this insightful blog on addressing data volume challenges during SAP S/4HANA conversions. Your emphasis on proactive data volume management strategies, such as archiving and deletion, highlights the importance of preparing the system for a smoother transition. Utilizing tools like the DVM App and SAP Solution Manager's DVM Workcenter can significantly aid in identifying and mitigating potential bottlenecks. The SAP Community's Data Volume Management page offers a wealth of resources and discussions on this topic for those interested in a deeper dive into data volume management practices. https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/data-volume-management

entry.title: Re: Can a theme be set default for a specific user by the...
entry.content:
Hi,

as far as I understand you can set a default theme for the users and you can deactivate the possibility for the users to choose an own theme. But you can do this only for all users, not for specific ones.

Kind regards
Thomas

entry.title: Reporting in GSTR1
entry.content:
Business has a scenario wherein GST is paid on advances received from customer. Such value of advances received and cleared subsequently against the billing documents has to be reported in the GSTR1 alongwith the annexure . Theses annexures comprises of the summary of these advances state wise and document wise. This data required is not available in the standard reports provided by the system and as this a statutory requirement request you to share the details of any SAP note or report available which would help for the reporting in GSTR1. Subsequently these details are also required for the GSTR3B as well. 

The sample GSTR1 is attached herewith wherein the fields required for reporting of advances value is highlighted on Page 4- Point 11A(1),11A(2),11B(1), 11B(2).


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Reporting in GSTR1
entry.content:
The statutory reports are generated using the Run Statutory Reports app. Once the reports (GSTR-1 and GSTR-3B) are successfully generated, they can be downloaded in the government-specified format.

The schema used in the reports is provided by the government and is included in the generated file.

Kindly note that we do not support adding any custom fields to the GSTR reports, as such modifications are not accepted by the GST portal during file upload.

 

Refer the below help document for your reference,

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/634261119fec4d58970471f2c4a9a740/c8289d53d3294345bd1a6d98f84e70ef.html?locale=en-US

 

Best regards,

SAP Support - S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: Re: Getting this error while trying to preview the app in...
entry.content:
Hi @nitish15,
a new version of the UI theme designer is deployed to SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment. Please try again and if you still get the error please open a ticket with component CA-UI2-THD.

entry.title: Prohibit changing the decision point
entry.content:
Good afternoon everyone.

I am a novice SAP PPM consultant, I am doing customization from scratch using ConfigurDtion Guide for (SAP Portfolio and Project Management in SAP S/4HANA 2022).
I have done all the customizations, but during testing I found that if I set the status for the initial decision point as Rejected or Canceled, the system automatically moves to the next decision point, which is not correct in my understanding.

I have tried different options to customize the status schema, but I have not been able to disallow going to the next DP.

Is it possible to implement this as a standard or is a special extension required?

Thank you very much in advance,
Kovalev Alexander.

entry.title: FSV with local CoA not displaying in Balance sheet application
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm encountering an issue regarding the FSV on S4 Hana Cloud Public version.

We have been creating an FSV of the YPCF CoA as shown below :

But when we try to use it in the Balance sheet/ Incoming statement app, we don't find the structure defined in the FSV created in the Manage Global Hierarchies app :

Do you have an explanation to it ?

Thank you in advance for your help.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 SAP Cloud Application Programming Model with SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Ajay,

I have registered for the 30th participant , please send the confirmation for the event.

Thanks,

entry.title: How to update the Documentation settings to resolve sequence/spilling of context over multiple pages
entry.content:
Hi all, 

For context, the current (default) sequencing when generating PDF/Word documentation for projects  (simulations) and books is the Step text/ Book page description first, then the screenshot second

This means that the text can appear at the bottom of one page, and the screenshot appears on the next page, which is impractical 

My questions

1. Is it possible to set up the documentation templates to ensure that each corresponding screenshot and its relevant text are displayed on a new page every time (to avoid the text and the relevant screenshot being split over two pages)

2. Is it possible to change the sequencing to the compound document template so that it follows the sequence of screenshot first, then the related text item (Step or description)

Thank you in advance

Stephen

entry.title: Re: SAP PRD QUERY
entry.content: The current state is that evey machine leaving factory is assigned one specific serial number in SAP. Now during the lifecycle, customer requires many of the parts. So while quoting the part to invoicing that part, how to link that machine serial in SAP so can get a report that serial wise what is the booking and revenue for parts sales.
